decision.overall_recommendation: VIX gate is open, so new entries are technically permitted, but this system's own tracked history is a serious red flag: 30 prior ranked-picks runs show only a 3.3% win rate, a -7.32% average return, and a 0% win rate specifically for rank-1 picks. That is effectively no edge, consistent with the underlying pullback-reversal screener being a real chart pattern that has NOT been statistically validated. Given that record, conviction across this entire list should be treated as low, sizes should not be pushed beyond the pre-computed 4%-risk amounts, and this run is better used as a monitoring/shortlist exercise than an aggressive buy program. Among the names, OXY, WMT, NYT, ZM, SFD and GMED show the cleanest combination of real earnings-beat streaks and constructive news; AHCO, BHF and HE show deteriorating fundamentals or consistent estimate-miss patterns and rank last regardless of their technical setups. Energy (CRGY, MUR, OXY) and the lone Technology name (ZM) add sector-concentration risk on top of an existing book that is already oil/tech-tilted (BNO, GCT). Ten of sixteen names report earnings within roughly three weeks, which is a real gap-risk consideration even though the earnings-buffer filter already screened for proximity.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: OXY beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters shown, including a huge May-2026 beat (1.06 vs 0.601 est), with Q1 2026 net income growth of +31.8% and EPS growth of +48.2% after a soft Q4 2025. News is dominated by Berkshire/Buffett building a large stake, the $9.7B OxyChem sale to Berkshire that accelerated debt reduction, and repeated analyst price-target hikes after beats, alongside genuine macro volatility (oil price swings, tariff/ceasefire headlines).
ranked_picks.rationale: Consistent EPS beats, an A- analyst rating with Buy consensus, and a real debt-reduction/Buffett-backed catalyst story outweigh the technical pattern alone; price sits just below POC (-0.91%), suggesting decent support underneath.
ranked_picks.bear_case: OXY's Q4 2025 net income and EPS actually declined (-0.88%/-1.1%), and the stock's recent moves have been heavily driven by volatile oil-price/geopolitical headlines rather than pure fundamentals, so a de-escalation in Middle East tensions could remove a key tailwind quickly.
flags: Earnings in ~20 days (2026-related date) - gap risk before thesis plays out
Energy-sector concentration if combined with CRGY/MUR picks and existing BNO (oil ETN) position

ranked_picks.research_highlight: WMT beat EPS estimates in 7 of the last 8 quarters (only Aug-2025 missed), with Q1 FY27 net income growth of +33.3%. News flow is dominated by strategic wins (Nasdaq-100 inclusion, $1T market cap, Google Gemini and OpenAI/ChatGPT shopping integrations) alongside real negatives (May-2026 guidance disappointment that dropped shares ~7%, a Walton family insider sale, and a $100M FTC settlement).
ranked_picks.rationale: A long, real beat streak plus a genuine AI/e-commerce growth narrative supports the pick, and price sits below its POC (-3.51%), indicating support underneath; Buy consensus (48 buy/15 hold/3 sell) backs this up.
ranked_picks.bear_case: The most recent quarter's guidance disappointed and triggered a real selloff, and repeated tariff-cost commentary from management suggests margin pressure risk that could resurface before the target is reached.
flags: Consumer Defensive sector - no direct overlap with existing Technology/Unknown positions

ranked_picks.research_highlight: NYT beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters, with Q4 2025 net income up +59% (though Q1 2026 showed a -32.3% net income decline). News includes Berkshire Hathaway initiating a stake in NYT, a new AI licensing deal with Amazon ($20-25M/year), 20% digital ad growth, and a BofA Neutral initiation flagging the rally may be 'largely priced in.'
ranked_picks.rationale: The consistent beat record plus a real institutional endorsement (Berkshire stake) and AI licensing monetization support the thesis beyond the chart pattern alone.
ranked_picks.bear_case: The most recent quarter (Q1 2026) showed a real revenue and net income decline, and at least one analyst has flagged valuation as already reflecting the good news, which could cap further upside.
flags: PriceVsPOCPct modestly above (+3.15%) - somewhat thinner support than names below POC

ranked_picks.research_highlight: ZM beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters shown, including consecutive 'beat-and-raise' prints, with AI Companion paid users up 184% and an 11-quarter revenue growth streak cited in news. Q1 FY2027 net income growth is negative (-36.9%), a reminder that beats have been against a low bar even as growth decelerates.
ranked_picks.rationale: A perfect beat streak and a real AI monetization story (Companion 2.0, Anthropic stake speculation, $1B buyback) support the pick, though the A+ quant rating sits oddly against a Hold analyst consensus (19 buy/27 hold/3 sell), worth noting explicitly.
ranked_picks.bear_case: Analysts have repeatedly flagged decelerating core growth despite the beats, and at least one high-profile commentator (Jim Cramer) has told investors to 'take a pass,' suggesting the growth story is not universally trusted even with the beat record intact.
flags: Technology-sector concentration relative to existing Technology position in current Webull book
Analyst consensus (Hold) conflicts with the A+ fundamentals score - worth flagging explicitly

ranked_picks.research_highlight: SFD beat EPS estimates in 4 of the last 6 reported quarters with estimates (missing in Aug-2025 and Mar-2025), and news is largely positive: a bullish Morgan Stanley initiation post-IPO, a raised annual profit outlook on hog-segment strength, and the $450M all-cash acquisition of Nathan's Famous.
ranked_picks.rationale: An A-rated, Buy-consensus name with real M&A-driven growth (Nathan's Famous) and a mostly-beat record supports the thesis independent of the technical setup.
ranked_picks.bear_case: Q1 2026 showed a real revenue (-10.1%) and net income (-25.1%) decline, and as a recently-IPO'd company (Jan 2025) SFD has a short public track record, limiting confidence in the durability of recent beats.
flags: Consumer Defensive - lower correlation to existing Technology/Unknown book

ranked_picks.research_highlight: GMED beat EPS estimates in 7 of the last 8 quarters (missing only May-2025), with a Buy consensus (26 buy/9 hold/1 sell) and an A rating. News flow is mostly routine analyst commentary with no major negative catalysts identified.
ranked_picks.rationale: A strong, consistent beat record and top-tier analyst rating support the pick even though the news itself is unremarkable rather than a specific positive catalyst.
ranked_picks.bear_case: Q1 2026 showed a real revenue decline (-8.1%) and net income decline (-11.6%), and with limited news coverage there is little independent confirmation of what is driving the recent bounce beyond the earnings beats themselves.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: SUPN beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters, often by wide margins (e.g., 0.92 vs 0.28 est in Feb-2026). However, News includes two real negatives: a downgrade on slower Qelbree prescription growth, and a failed Phase 2b depression trial (SPN-820) that missed its primary endpoint; the $795M Sage Therapeutics/Zurzuvae acquisition is a positive offsetting catalyst.
ranked_picks.rationale: The magnitude and consistency of the beats is unusually strong, and the Sage acquisition adds a real pipeline catalyst, but this is balanced against genuine clinical and analyst setbacks.
ranked_picks.bear_case: A failed Phase 2b trial and an analyst downgrade on core-drug prescription deceleration are real, company-specific negatives that could resurface as the SPN-820 program's future gets clarified.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: MWA beat EPS estimates in 6 of 8 trailing quarters, with the latest quarter (Q2 FY26) showing strong revenue (+20.8%) and net income (+36.8%) growth. News is sparse but includes a positive $3.4B 2026-2030 infrastructure investment plan announcement.
ranked_picks.rationale: A solid beat record combined with a real long-term capex growth plan supports the thesis, even though news volume and analyst commentary are limited (Hold consensus, A- rating).
ranked_picks.bear_case: Q1 FY26 showed a real revenue and net income decline (-16.4%/-17.9%), and with thin news coverage there is little independent confirmation beyond the earnings numbers themselves.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: CRGY beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters, but the underlying fundamentals score is weak (overall score 2, C rating, poor ROE/ROA/debt-to-equity scores). Q1 2026 revenue grew +36.7% but net income fell -47.5%. The dominant recent catalyst is the $3.1B all-stock acquisition of Vital Energy, plus at least one analyst downgrade.
ranked_picks.rationale: The beat streak is real, and the Vital Energy merger is a substantive catalyst, but the weak underlying quant fundamentals (C rating) temper conviction relative to higher-quality names above it.
ranked_picks.bear_case: Net income fell sharply in the most recent quarter despite the EPS beat, and the all-stock Vital Energy acquisition carries integration/dilution risk that the technical pattern does not price in.
flags: PriceVsPOCPct well below POC (-17.68%) is favorable for support
Energy-sector concentration with MUR/OXY and existing BNO (oil ETN) position

ranked_picks.research_highlight: MDU beat EPS estimates in 5 of 8 trailing quarters, with a positive Q1 2026 (revenue +13.5%, net income +5.9%). News includes a reinstated Buy rating from BofA and a newly announced $3.4B 2026-2030 infrastructure plan, both constructive.
ranked_picks.rationale: A reasonably solid beat record and a fresh, sizable capex plan support the thesis, though the WeakRR flag on this trade plan is a real drag on the risk/reward profile.
ranked_picks.bear_case: The stock's R:R already fell short of the system's minimum threshold after support/resistance refinement, meaning the reward on offer is thinner than most other picks even before considering fundamentals.
flags: WeakRR: true - R:R fell short of minimum after refinement, weakest reward/risk profile in this batch

ranked_picks.research_highlight: MUR beat EPS estimates in 6 of the last 7 quarters (one miss in Jan-2025), including a large Jan-2026 beat (0.14 vs -0.08 est). News is mostly positive (analyst upgrades, oil-price-driven energy rallies), but the analyst consensus is only Hold (9 buy/25 hold/2 sell) despite the beat record.
ranked_picks.rationale: The beat record is solid, but the extreme 34.29:1 R:R is flagged by the system itself as more likely reflecting an unusually tight stop than an unusually good target, so this should not be read as an unusually attractive setup.
ranked_picks.bear_case: The heavily Hold-skewed analyst consensus despite good beats suggests the Street sees limited additional upside already priced in, and the StopSanityFlag means the reward math here is less trustworthy than it appears.
flags: StopSanityFlag: true - R:R of 34.29:1 likely reflects an unusually tight stop, not an unusually good target
Energy-sector concentration with CRGY/OXY and existing BNO (oil ETN) position

ranked_picks.research_highlight: ASO has an inconsistent record - beating estimates in 4 of 8 trailing quarters and missing in 4, with FY guidance cut multiple times in 2024/2025 amid tariff pressures, though it also raised guidance more recently on Nike/Jordan momentum. Q1 2026 showed a real revenue decline (-16.1%) and net income decline (-60.6%).
ranked_picks.rationale: The A rating and Buy consensus reflect decent balance-sheet quality, but the genuinely inconsistent beat/miss pattern and repeated guidance cuts warrant a lower rank than names with cleaner earnings execution.
ranked_picks.bear_case: The company has missed estimates in half of its last 8 quarters and cut guidance multiple times citing tariffs, a real execution inconsistency that the clean technical setup does not resolve.
flags: Earnings in ~54 days - lower near-term gap risk than most peers

ranked_picks.research_highlight: AEO's beat record is mixed - 5 beats, 2 misses, 1 in-line across the last 8 quarters, with a real guidance withdrawal in May-2025 ('clearly disappointed with our execution') and a large recent miss in Mar-2026 versus estimates. News is dominated by viral Sydney Sweeney/Travis Kelce marketing campaigns that boosted sales and stock price sharply, but also tariff cost hits (~$70M) and reports of the marketing 'effect wearing off.'
ranked_picks.rationale: The stock has genuine positive catalysts (celebrity marketing driving real customer growth) but also a real recent earnings miss and withdrawn guidance episode, making this a higher-risk, higher-volatility pick versus cleaner names above it.
ranked_picks.bear_case: AEO withdrew full-year guidance once already in 2025 and missed its most recent reported quarter (0.50 vs 0.71 est), and multiple articles note the marketing-driven sales boost may be fading, undermining the durability of the current bounce.
flags: Earnings in ~48 days - lower near-term gap risk
PriceVsPOCPct notably above POC (+5.74%) - thinner volume support underneath than names below POC

ranked_picks.research_highlight: HE has missed EPS estimates in 5 of the last 8 quarters, including a large miss in May-2026 (0.18 vs 0.27 est). The analyst consensus, while labeled Hold, is actually skewed bearish (0 buy/8 hold/5 sell), and news includes a dilutive secondary share offering that pressured the stock.
ranked_picks.rationale: The consistent pattern of missed estimates and a genuinely bearish-skewed analyst base (5 sells, 0 buys) are real fundamental negatives that argue against this pick despite qualifying on the technical screen.
ranked_picks.bear_case: A recurring miss pattern combined with an analyst base that is effectively bearish (0 buy ratings, 5 sells) suggests the Street does not see near-term upside, and lingering wildfire-liability overhang adds ongoing uncertainty.
flags: Analyst consensus notably bearish (0 buy/8 hold/5 sell) despite Hold label - conflicts with a bullish trade thesis

ranked_picks.research_highlight: BHF has missed EPS estimates in 6 of the last 8 quarters, including large misses in Feb-2026 (3.93 vs 5.19 est) and Aug-2025 (3.43 vs 4.70 est). The analyst consensus is only 1 buy against 16 hold and 3 sell - a notably weak skew - though news includes speculation the company is exploring a sale, which lifted the stock in early 2025.
ranked_picks.rationale: A recurring pattern of missed estimates and a very thin bull case among analysts (just 1 buy rating) make this one of the weaker fundamental stories in the batch, despite a reasonable R:R on the trade plan.
ranked_picks.bear_case: Six of the last eight quarters missed EPS estimates, and with only a single Buy rating on Wall Street, there is little analyst conviction behind a continued move higher; insurance-company GAAP earnings here have also been extremely lumpy (e.g., -659% net income growth in Q1 2026), making the fundamental picture hard to read cleanly.
flags: Analyst consensus notably weak (1 buy/16 hold/3 sell)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: AHCO has missed EPS estimates in 4 of the last 5 reported quarters, including a negative EPS surprise in May-2026 (-0.06 vs 0.0125 est). Recent news includes a disclosed cyberattack/material breach involving patient data and insurance billing passwords, a genuine negative catalyst, alongside prior analyst forecast cuts on weak sales.
ranked_picks.rationale: This is the weakest fundamental story in the batch - a clear recent pattern of missed estimates plus a fresh, material cybersecurity incident - and it is ranked last despite qualifying on the technical screen; the StopSanityFlag also means the reward math is less trustworthy than it appears.
ranked_picks.bear_case: A recent cyberattack involving patient and billing data is a real, ongoing operational and reputational risk on top of a deteriorating earnings trend (4 of 5 recent quarters missed), making this the highest-risk pick in the list regardless of the clean chart pattern.
flags: StopSanityFlag: true - R:R of 24.62:1 likely reflects an unusually tight stop, not an unusually good target
PriceVsPOCPct notably above POC (+11.91%) - thinner volume support underneath
